channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania’s DCEA destroys over 500kg of seized heroin in Mtwara anti-drug operation
item.description: Tanzanian authorities under the Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) have successfully destroyed 504 kilograms of heroin in the Mtwara region as part of ongoing efforts to fight drug traffick...

item.title: BTS Suga participates in 12K marathon ahead of California 'Arirang' concert
item.description: BTS member Suga, also known as Min Yoongi, made headlines after reportedly taking part in a 12-kilometre marathon in California shortly before his scheduled appearance at the ‘Arirang’ concert. Accord...

item.title: Tinubu Emerges APC Presidential Candidate, Set to Receive Certificate on Sunday
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has emerged as the presidential candidate of the All Progressives Congress (APC) following the party’s presidential primary exercise ahead of the 2027 general elections. Accordin...

item.title: Court orders Sipho Ngwenya to repay R30 million to Ingonyama Trust after failed appeal
item.description: Former Ingonyama Trust Board chairperson Sipho Ngwenya has been ordered by the Pietermaritzburg High Court to repay R30 million to the Ingonyama Trust following a legal dispute over the transfer of fu...

item.title: Police identify suspect in Mississauga fatal stabbing investigation
item.description: Peel Regional Police have identified a suspect in connection with a fatal stabbing that occurred in Mississauga. The investigation centres on a violent incident that resulted in the death of a woman, ...

item.title: Lena Dunham says husband Luis Felber did not recognise her from Girls when they first met
item.description: Lena Dunham has spoken candidly about the early days of her relationship with British musician Luis Felber, revealing that he did not recognise her as the creator and star of the HBO series Girls when...

item.title: Scientific review links sleep quality to the brain’s removal of Alzheimer’s-related waste
item.description: A new scientific review published in the journal Science has highlighted the important role sleep plays in helping the brain remove toxic waste products associated with neurological diseases such as A...

item.title: Marco Rubio meets PM Modi, discusses India-US ties, energy security and possible Modi visit to the US
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi and conveyed an invitation from US President Donald Trump for Modi to visit the United States in the near future. The me...

item.title: Breakdown of Sonko–Diomaye Political Alliance and Emerging Power Struggle in Senegal
item.description: The article examines the growing political tension between Senegal’s President Bassirou Diomaye Faye and Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, who were once seen as a strong united leadership under the PASTEF...

item.title: Elderly tourists brutally murdered in Kruger National Park as police launch manhunt
item.description: Limpopo police have launched a manhunt for suspects after two elderly tourists were found murdered in the Kruger National Park. The victims, a 71-year-old white male and a white female whose identity ...

item.title: Tanzania Pharmacy Council to End Recognition of One-Year Dispensers from December 2026
item.description: The Tanzania Pharmacy Council has announced an important policy change affecting graduates of the one-year pharmaceutical dispensing programme, stating that it will stop recognising this cadre startin...

item.title: Scouts and Volunteers Place 90,000 Flags at Los Angeles National Cemetery for Memorial Day
item.description: Hundreds of Scouts from the Western Los Angeles County Council, Scouting America, along with veteran groups and community volunteers, gathered at the Los Angeles National Cemetery to take part in a lo...

item.title: Ella Blackamore’s endometriosis journey highlights delayed diagnosis and medical dismissal of severe period pain
item.description: Ella Blackamore’s experience with endometriosis highlights the significant challenges many women face in receiving timely diagnosis and appropriate care for chronic pelvic pain conditions. She began m...

item.title: US–Iran ceasefire talks progress as Trump signals possible strikes amid West Asia tensions
item.description: The live updates report escalating but simultaneously evolving diplomatic and military developments in the ongoing West Asia conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States. According to the la...

item.title: FBI launches national fitness competition to identify its most physically capable agents
item.description: The FBI has announced a new national initiative aimed at identifying the most physically fit male and female agents across its 56 field offices. According to reports, the programme will culminate in a...

item.title: PRP Clears Donald Duke and Two Others for Presidential Primary Contest
item.description: The Peoples Redemption Party (PRP) has officially cleared three aspirants to contest for its presidential primary ahead of the party’s upcoming election. The approved candidates include Dr. Nnaoke Ufe...

item.title: Papa Penny explains resignation from MK Party and joins Floyd Shivambu’s movement
item.description: Gezane “Papa Penny” Kobane, a veteran South African musician and former Member of Parliament for the MK Party, has publicly explained the reasons behind his resignation from the party. Speaking in an ...

item.title: Toronto police investigate armed robbery involving over $1 million in stolen jewellery and four suspects
item.description: Toronto police are investigating a violent armed robbery involving jewellery valued at more than $1 million and are seeking four suspects in connection with the incident. According to investigators, t...

item.title: Early Behind-the-Scenes Photos of Audrey Hepburn in Monte Carlo (1951) and Her Path to Roman Holiday
item.description: The article recounts a behind-the-scenes story from 1951 involving photographer Edward Quinn and a young, then-unknown Audrey Hepburn during the production of the film ‘Monte Carlo Baby.’ Quinn descri...

item.title: Cayetano Defends Duterte’s Anti-Drug Campaign as a Measure to Protect Communities
item.description: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano defended former President Rodrigo Duterte’s controversial war on drugs, describing it as a “pro-life campaign” aimed at protecting ordinary Filipinos from the harmful effec...
